message:
fix(scheduler): replace timer ID saturation with wrap-around allocation

Previously, `set_timeout` used `saturating_add` for the timer ID counter,
which meant that once `next_timer_id` reached `u64::MAX` it would stay
pinned there and every subsequent timer would receive the same ID. This
made it impossible to independently cancel timers created after the
counter saturated.

Extract a new `allocate_timer_id` method that:
1. Returns the next sequential ID when below u64::MAX (fast path)
2. On overflow, resets `next_timer_id` to 1 and recycles u64::MAX
3. Falls back to linear scan of in-use IDs if u64::MAX is still live
4. Checks both the timer heap and cancelled set via `timer_id_in_use`

Update the existing saturation test to verify wrap-around behavior
(second timer now gets ID 1 instead of a duplicate u64::MAX) and add
a new test `scheduler_timer_id_wrap_preserves_cancellation_semantics`
proving both the max-ID and wrapped-ID timers can be independently
cancelled and that no phantom firings occur after cancellation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
